title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: contributors maintainers project, pledge respect people contribute reporting issues, posting feature requests, updating documentation, submitting pull requests patches, activities. committed making participation project harassment-free experience everyone, regardless level experience, gender, gender identity expression, sexual orientation, disability, personal appearance, body size, race, ethnicity, age, religion. Examples unacceptable behavior participants include use sexual language imagery, derogatory comments personal attacks, trolling, public private harassment, insults, unprofessional conduct. Project maintainers right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct. Project maintainers follow Code Conduct may removed project team. Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported opening issue contacting one project maintainers. Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant (https://www.contributor-covenant.org), version 1.0.0, available https://contributor-covenant.org/version/1/0/0/.

what: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegHO
title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegHO
text: ggseg 2.0 atlas package providing brain atlas data ggseg ecosystem. data-package custom functions.

title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegHO
text: data/*.rda — Unified ggseg_atlas objects (2D-atlas) R/data.R — Roxygen2 documentation atlas R/ggsegHO-package.R — Package-level docs, imports ggseg.formats R/sysdata.rda — Internal palette data (brain_pals) data-raw/ — Scripts generated/converted atlas data

title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegHO
text: package depends ggseg.formats provides ggseg_atlas class. atlas objects work ggseg::geom_brain() (2D).

title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegHO
text: Update docs: devtools::document() Run tests: devtools::test() (testthat edition 3, describe/style) Full check: devtools::check() Build pkgdown site: pkgdown::build_site()

title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegHO
text: code comments except necessary workarounds Roxygen2 markdown enabled Tests use describe/BDD style vdiffr visual regression NEWS.md tracks user-facing changes (CI/pkgdown changes) URLs point ggseg GitHub org (LCBC-UiO)

what: ggsegHO: Harvard-Oxford Atlas for the 'ggseg' Ecosystem — ggsegHO-package
title: ggsegHO: Harvard-Oxford Atlas for the 'ggseg' Ecosystem — ggsegHO-package
text: Harvard-Oxford cortical, subcortical, cerebellar atlases 'ggseg' ecosystem. Includes original Harvard-Oxford atlas (FSL) Harvard-Oxford Atlas 2.0 (HOA-2, Rushmore et al. 2022) 'ggseg_atlas' objects 2D polygon 3D mesh geometry.

title: ggsegHO: Harvard-Oxford Atlas for the 'ggseg' Ecosystem — ggsegHO-package
text: Maintainer: Athanasia Mo Mowinckel .m.mowinckel@psykologi.uio.(ORCID) Authors: Didac Vidal-Pineiro d.v.pineiro@psykologi.uio.(ORCID)

title: Harvard-Oxford Atlas 2.0 Cerebellar — ho2_cereb
text: Cerebellar segmentation Harvard-Oxford Atlas 2.0 (HOA-2), including cerebellar cortex white matter. Contains 2D polygon geometry ggseg::geom_brain().

title: Harvard-Oxford Atlas 2.0 Cerebellar — ho2_cereb
text: ggseg.formats::ggseg_atlas object (cerebellar).

title: Harvard-Oxford Atlas 2.0 Cerebellar — ho2_cereb
text: Rushmore RJ, et al. (2022). Frontiers Neuroanatomy 16:1035420. (doi:10.3389/fnana.2022.1035420 )

title: Harvard-Oxford Atlas 2.0 Cerebellar — ho2_cereb
code: ho2_cereb() #>  #> ── ho2_cereb ggseg atlas ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: cerebellar #> Regions: 4 #> Hemispheres: left, right #> Views: flatmap #> Palette: ✔ #> Rendering: ✔ ggseg #> ✔ ggseg3d (vertices) #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   hemi  region                        label                               #>   <chr> <chr>                         <chr>                               #> 1 left  Cerebellar_Cortex_Left        left_Cerebellar_Cortex_Left         #> 2 right Cerebellar_Cortex_Right       right_Cerebellar_Cortex_Right       #> 3 left  Cerebellar_White_Matter_Left  left_Cerebellar_White_Matter_Left   #> 4 right Cerebellar_White_Matter_Right right_Cerebellar_White_Matter_Right plot(ho2_cereb())

title: Harvard-Oxford Atlas 2.0 Cortical — ho2_cort
text: Cortical parcellation 49 regions per hemisphere Harvard-Oxford Atlas 2.0 (HOA-2), based 50 HCP subjects. Contains 2D polygon geometry ggseg::geom_brain() 3D vertex indices ggseg3d::ggseg3d().

title: Harvard-Oxford Atlas 2.0 Subcortical — ho2_sub
text: Subcortical segmentation 19 structures Harvard-Oxford Atlas 2.0 (HOA-2), based 100 HCP subjects. Contains 2D polygon geometry ggseg::geom_brain() 3D mesh data ggseg3d::ggseg3d().

title: Harvard-Oxford Atlas 2.0 Subcortical — ho2_sub
text: ggseg.formats::ggseg_atlas object (subcortical).

title: Harvard-Oxford Atlas 2.0 Subcortical — ho2_sub
text: Rushmore RJ, et al. (2022). Frontiers Neuroanatomy 16:1035420. (doi:10.3389/fnana.2022.1035420 )

title: Harvard-Oxford Atlas 2.0 Subcortical — ho2_sub
code: ho2_sub() #>  #> ── ho2_sub ggseg atlas ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: subcortical #> Regions: 19 #> Hemispheres: left, right, NA #> Views: axial_2, axial_3, axial_4, axial_5, coronal_3, coronal_4, coronal_5, #> sagittal, axial_1, axial_6, coronal_1, coronal_2 #> Palette: ✔ #> Rendering: ✔ ggseg #> ✔ ggseg3d (meshes) #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> # A tibble: 19 × 3 #>    hemi  region                      label                       #>    <chr> <chr>                       <chr>                       #>  1 left  nucleus accumbens left      Nucleus_Accumbens_Left      #>  2 right nucleus accumbens right     Nucleus_Accumbens_Right     #>  3 left  caudate left                Caudate_Left                #>  4 right caudate right               Caudate_Right               #>  5 left  putamen left                Putamen_Left                #>  6 right putamen right               Putamen_Right               #>  7 left  globus pallidus left        Globus_Pallidus_Left        #>  8 right globus pallidus right       Globus_Pallidus_Right       #>  9 NA    brainstem                   Brainstem                   #> 10 left  thalamus left               Thalamus_Left               #> 11 right thalamus right              Thalamus_Right              #> 12 left  hippocampal formation left  Hippocampal_Formation_Left  #> 13 right hippocampal formation right Hippocampal_Formation_Right #> 14 left  amygdala left               Amygdala_Left               #> 15 right amygdala right              Amygdala_Right              #> 16 left  vdc anterior left           VDC_Anterior_Left           #> 17 right vdc anterior right          VDC_Anterior_Right          #> 18 left  vdc posterior left          VDC_Posterior_Left          #> 19 right vdc posterior right         VDC_Posterior_Right         plot(ho2_sub())

text: Subcortical segmentation Harvard-Oxford atlas distributed FSL. Contains 2D polygon geometry ggseg::geom_brain() 3D mesh data ggseg3d::ggseg3d().

title: Harvard-Oxford Subcortical Atlas — hoSub
text: ggseg.formats::ggseg_atlas object (subcortical).

title: Harvard-Oxford Subcortical Atlas — hoSub
text: Makris, et al. (2006) Schizophrenia research 83(2-3):155-151 (doi:10.1016/j.schres.2005.11.020 )

title: Harvard-Oxford Subcortical Atlas — hoSub
code: hoSub() #>  #> ── ho_subcortical ggseg atlas ────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: subcortical #> Regions: 6 #> Hemispheres: left, NA, right #> Views: axial_1, axial_2, axial_3, axial_4, axial_5, coronal_1, coronal_2, #> coronal_3, coronal_4, sagittal #> Palette: ✔ #> Rendering: ✔ ggseg #> ✔ ggseg3d (meshes) #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> # A tibble: 11 × 3 #>    hemi  region            label                   #>    <chr> <chr>             <chr>                   #>  1 left  lateral ventricle Left_Lateral_Ventricle  #>  2 left  thalamus          Left_Thalamus           #>  3 NA    brain stem        Brain-Stem              #>  4 left  hippocampus       Left_Hippocampus        #>  5 left  amygdala          Left_Amygdala           #>  6 left  accumbens         Left_Accumbens          #>  7 right lateral ventricle Right_Lateral_Ventricle #>  8 right thalamus          Right_Thalamus          #>  9 right hippocampus       Right_Hippocampus       #> 10 right amygdala          Right_Amygdala          #> 11 right accumbens         Right_Accumbens         plot(hoSub())

title: ggsegHO 2.0.0
text: hoCort now ggseg_atlas object (ggseg.formats) containing 2D data. 2D-atlas (3D mesh data available). Use ggplot() + ggseg::geom_brain(atlas = hoCort) 2D plots. ggseg.formats now hard dependency (Depends). Package URLs updated LCBC-UiO ggseg GitHub organisation.

title: ggsegHO 1.0.02
text: updated atlases work ggseg >= 1.6.0

title: ggsegHO 1.0.01
text: Added NEWS.md file track changes package.
